Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

STT 2, 179 [Udug-hul Compendium][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[...] a-la₂ lu₂ ⸢an⸣-[dul]

(o 1) [...] (Akkadian: binding)! The alû-demon (Akkadian: who) covers the man [...] ... this is the binding!

[... ka]-sa-a-ti!(KA) a-lu-u ša₂ LU₂ i-kat₃-[ta-mu]
o 2o 2

[...] ⸢AM₃⸣ niŋ₂-la-⸢am₃⸣

[...] ka-sa-a-⸢ti⸣
o 3o 3

[... kur]-ra-⸢ke₄⸣

(o 3) Bondages of the land [...] (that) provoke sickness for the people.

[ka-ma]-⸢a⸣-tu₂ ša₂ ma-⸢a⸣-[ti]
o 4o 4

[...] ⸢gig⸣-ga nam-lu₂-⸢u₁₈-lu⸣-[ke₄]

[mu]-⸢šam⸣-ri-ṣa-tu ša₂ ni-[ši]
o 5o 5

[... niŋ₂]-⸢ḫulu⸣-dim₂-ma pa₄-ḫal-la-⸢ke₄⸣

(o 5) The evil [...] (Akkadian: eye) of the restless one.

[e]-nu le-mut-tu₂ <<a-na>> mu-tal-[li-ki]1
o 6o 6

⸢ub⸣-še₃ <igi> ab-ši-in-bar ub im-⸢sug₄⸣

(o 6) (The evil eye) looked into the corner (Akkadian: and) emptied the corner; looked into the hiding places (Akkadian: and) emptied the hiding places; looked into the chamber of the land (Akkadian: and) emptied the chamber of the land.

a-na ⸢tub₂⸣-qi₂ ip-pa-lis-ma tub₂-qi₂ u₂-⸢riq⸣
o 7o 7

da-še₃ <igi> ab-⸢ši⸣-in-bar da im-⸢sug₄⸣

a-na ša₂-ḫa-tu₂ ip-pa-lis-ma ša₂-ḫa-tu₂ u₂-riq
o 8o 8

ama₅ kalam-ma-še₃ <igi> ⸢ab⸣-ši-in-bar ⸢ama₅⸣ kalam im-sug₄

ana maš-tak ⸢ma⸣-a-tu₂ ip-pa-lis-ma maš-tak ma-a-ti u₂-riq
o 9o 9

lu₂-u₁₉-lu pa₄-ḫal-la-še₃ <igi> ⸢ab⸣-ši-in-bar ŋeš ḫaš kud-ra-gin₇ gu₂ ki-⸢a im⸣-[mi-in-gurum]

(o 9) (The evil eye) looked upon the restless man (Akkadian: and) bent (Akkadian: his) neck to the ground1 like a felled, a broken tree.

ana LU₂ ⸢mut-tal⸣-<li>-ku ip-pa-lis-ma ki-ma GIŠ nak-si šeb-ri ⸢ki-šad-su iq?⸣-[ta-du]-ud
o 10o 10

den-ki lu₂-bi igi u-me-ni-in-duḫ

(o 10) Enki (Akkadian: Ea) looked at the man (Akkadian: him and) put some bread on his head;

de₂-a šu₂-a-⸢tu₂⸣ e-mur-ma2
o 11o 11

ninda saŋ-ŋa₂-na mu-⸢un⸣-ni-ŋar

a-ka-la ⸢ina⸣ qaq-qa-di-šu₂ iš-kun
o 12o 12

ninda su-an-ni mu-un-in-teŋ₃

(o 12) he brought bread over his body.

a-ka-la ina ⸢zu⸣-mur-ri-šu₂ u₂-ṭaḫ-ḫi
o 13o 13

šud₃ nam-ti-la-ke₄ mu-un-⸢ne⸣-šud₃

(o 13) He will utter for him the prayer of life:

ik-rib TI.LA i-kar-rab-šu₂
o 14o 14

[lu₂]-u₁₉-lu dumu diŋir-⸢ra-an⸣-na za-e-me-en

(o 14) "Man, you are the son of (your personal) god!2 The bread that I brought over your head, the bread with which I wiped your body, may it cool down your illness (Akkadian: and) you, recover!

a-me-lu DUMU [DINGIR]-šu₂ at-ta
o 15o 15

ninda saŋ-zu im-mi-⸢in⸣-teŋ₃-a-ta

a-ka-la ša₂ [ina] ⸢qaq⸣-qa-di-ka u₂-ṭa-ḫu-u
o 16o 16

ninda su-zu im-mi-in-su-ub-ba-ke₄

a-ka-la ša₂ ⸢zu⸣-mur-ka u₂-kap-pir-ra
o 17o 17

tu-ra-zu ḫe₂-en-sig-⸢ga⸣ za-e-me-en til₃-la

mu-ru-uṣ-ka lip-⸢šaḫ⸣-ma at-ta bu-luṭ3
o 18o 18

ki nam-ti-la-ke₄ ŋiri₃-zu gub-bu-da

(o 18) Place your foot on the ground of life!

ina qaq-qar ba-la₂-ṭi GIR₃-ka šu-kun
o 19o 19

lu₂ dumu diŋir-ra-an-na za-e-me-en

(o 19) Man, you are the son of (your personal) god".

a-me-lu DUMU DINGIR-šu₂!(U) at-ta
Reverse
r 1r 1

igi ⸢lu₂ gig⸣-ga <igi> mu-⸢un⸣-ši-bar-ra-zu

(r 1) The eye, that looked at you with malevolence;

e-nu ša₂ ana ma-ru-⸢uš⸣-ti ip-pal-su-ka
r 2r 2

igi niŋ-ḫulu-dim₂-ma <igi> [mu]-un-ši-bar-ra-zu

(r 2) the eye that looked at you with evil;

e-nu ša₂ ana le-mut-⸢ti⸣ ip-pal-su-ka
r 3r 3

⸢lu₂⸣ iri-bar-ra-ke₄ <igi> mu-un-ši-in-bar-ra-zu

(r 3) which (Akkadian: either) looked at you in the city's hiding places;

lu-u ša₂ ina ša₂-ḫa-⸢ti⸣ IRI ip-pal-su-ka
r 4r 4

lu₂ iri-šag₄-ga-ke₄ <igi> mu-un-ši-in-bar-ra-zu

(r 4) (Akkadian: or) which looked at you in the city's centre.

lu-u ša₂ ina qe₂-reb ⸢IRI⸣ ip-pal-su-ka
r 5r 5

dnin-tin-ug₅-ga gi-⸢na⸣ ḫu-mu-un-du₃

(r 5) May Nintinuga (Akkadian: Gula) prick it with a reed!

dGU.LA ina ⸢qa⸣-ne₂-e lis-ḫi-il-šu₂
r 6r 6

dda-mu dur₁₀!(SU-)⸢al⸣ ḫe₂-en-sag₃-ga

(r 6) May Damu beat it with an axe!

dMIN<(da-mu)> ina ⸢pal⸣-ti lim-ḫa-as-su
r 7r 7

dgu-nu-ra ⸢dimgul⸣ gal-bi ḫu-mu-un-da-<ra>

(r 7) May Gunura impale it with a big pole!

dMIN<(gu-nu-ra)> ina der-⸢kul⸣-[li] ra-be₂-e li-ir-ti-šu₂
r 8r 8

šeŋ₃ an-na ⸢šeŋ₃⸣-gin₇ ki-a <šu> mu-un-<ši>-in-bar-ra-zu

(r 8) Like the rain that flows from the sky (Akkadian: and) is released ontothe earth, may Enki (Akkadian: Ea), king of the Abzu, tear (it) out from your body!

ki-ma zu-⸢un⸣-ni ša₂ ul-te <ANe> šur-du-ma ana er-ṣe-tu₂ uš-šu-ru
r 9r 9

su-bar-ra-zu-⸢ta⸣ den-ki lugal abzu-ke₄ ḫe₂-em-ma-an-zi-zi

ina zu-um-ri-⸢ka de₂⸣-a LUGAL ap-si-i li-is-suḫ
r 1010

lu-u₁₉-lu-⸢bi ḫe₂-en⸣-sikil-la ḫe₂-en-dadag-ga

(r 10) May that man become pure, may he become clean!

r 1111

šu sag₁₀-ga diŋir-ra-na-še₃ ḫe₂-en-ši-in-gi₄-gi₄

(r 11) May he return to the hand of his favourable god!

(colophon)
r 1212

[... mmu]-šal-lim—aš-šur!(AŠ)

(r 12) [...] Mušallim-Aššur3 [...] its original [...] he shall live long [...].

r 1313

LIBIR?-⸢BI?⸣ [...]

r 1414

SUMUNbar?-ra!(.SU) [...]

1"ana" is not on all versions (see Schramm 2008: 133). I regarded "ana" as a scribal mistake, because the translation of the Sumerian line is better without it. On the other hand, the meaning implied by "ana" (the evil eye "on" the restless one) better fits with the rest of the text, especially obv. 9 and rev. 2 ss.

2The other versions have "amēlu šuātu" (See Schramm 2008: 135). I don't regard the omission of "amēlu" as a scribal mistake, because because "šuātu" alone also makes sense.

3sig may be a variant or a scribal mistake for šed on this line.

1 The Akkadian version omits the word for "ground".

2 The Akkadian version has "his god " instead of "your god".

3 If correctly read , this may be the eponym official for the year 639 * BC ; alternatively it may be a defective writing for the Huzirina scribe Mušallim-Bau.

Created by Marie-Françoise Besnier for the AHRC-funded GKAB Project, 2010 and released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/cams/gkab/P338498
